channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Stay Free Alberta appeals ruling that rejected their separation petition
item.description: Stay Free Alberta, an advocacy group based in Alberta, has filed an appeal challenging the recent Court of King’s Bench decision that struck down their petition seeking separation from the province. T...

item.title: Aaron Rai becomes first English winner of US PGA Championship since 1919
item.description: England's Aaron Rai made history at the US PGA Championship at Aronimink, claiming his first major title. The 31-year-old became the first Englishman in over a century to lift the Wanamaker Trophy, th...

item.title: Arkansas Man Arrested for Threatening Walmart Shooting Over Hantavirus Concerns
item.description: Aaron Bynum, a 20-year-old man from Oakland, Arkansas, was arrested after allegedly threatening to carry out a mass shooting at a local Walmart if the country went into lockdown due to a hantavirus ou...

item.title: Tribal Chief and Two Others Killed in Wana IED Explosion
item.description: In a tragic incident on Monday morning, an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in the bustling Rustam Bazaar area of Wana, resulting in the deaths of three people, including Malik Tariq Wazir,...

item.title: Bulgaria Claims First Eurovision Victory Amid Boycotts Over Israel Participation
item.description: Bulgaria won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest held in Vienna, Austria, with Dara performing the song 'Bangaranga.' This marks Bulgaria's first-ever win in the contest, which featured 25 acts following...

item.title: Jannik Sinner Wins Italian Open, Secures €1m and Golden Masters
item.description: Jannik Sinner don make history as e win the Italian Open on Sunday, beating Casper Ruud 6-4, 6-4. With this win, Sinner don secure him sixth consecutive Masters 1000 tournament title and bag €1,007,16...

item.title: Vehicle flips on its side in North York collision; no serious injuries reported
item.description: A vehicle flipped onto its side after a collision in North York on May 17, 2026. Fortunately, authorities report that no one sustained serious injuries as a result of the incident. Emergency services ...

item.title: Durban residents challenge municipal approvals over noisy padel courts
item.description: In Durban’s Morningside suburb, residents are taking legal action against the noise generated by nearby padel courts at the NMJ Islamic Centre. Usha Neerpath and Naseer Abdullah say the constant thud ...

item.title: Zipf’s Law and the Hidden Inequalities in South Africa’s Urban Landscape
item.description: In South Africa, applying Zipf’s Law to the country’s urban hierarchy gives a misleading impression of spatial and economic normality. Zipf’s Law, which predicts a smooth rank-size distribution among ...

item.title: Cubana Chief Priest Explains Why He Didn’t Contest APC Primaries in Imo
item.description: Okechukwu Pascal, wey people sabi as Cubana Chief Priest, don talk finally about di APC House of Representatives primaries for Orsu/Orlu/Oru East Federal Constituency for Imo State. E clear say e volu...

item.title: William P. Welsh’s Art Deco Posters from the 1930s Showcase Commercial and Artistic Mastery
item.description: William P. Welsh (1889–1984) was a distinguished American illustrator and painter whose career spanned multiple decades, leaving a lasting impact on mid-20th-century advertising and visual media. Know...

item.title: Marie-Philip Poulin Leads Montreal Victoire One Win from Walter Cup Title
item.description: Montreal Victoire captain Marie-Philip Poulin has been instrumental in her team's quest for their first Walter Cup. In Game 2 of the finals against the Ottawa Charge, Poulin made a decisive no-look pa...

item.title: Prospective Changes to Constitution Could Shift Key Powers Back to Federal Government
item.description: The latest discussions in Pakistani political circles suggest that a so-called '28th Amendment' or legislative package may be under consideration, even though both PPP and PML-N publicly deny any form...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Middle East Tensions Following UAE Nuclear Plant Attack
item.description: Oil prices surged to their highest levels in two weeks after a drone attack targeted the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, causing fresh concerns over Middle East stability. Bre...

item.title: Labour Leadership Contenders Spark Criticism Over EU Rejoining Comments
item.description: Fears of a Brexit reversal have intensified following statements from Labour's leading leadership candidates, Andy Burnham and Wes Streeting, suggesting Britain should eventually rejoin the European U...

item.title: WHO Continues to Assess Hantavirus as Low-Risk Threat
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has maintained its assessment that hantavirus currently poses a low risk to global public health. This statement comes amid ongoing monitoring of the virus, which i...

item.title: Meta introduces parental supervision tools for teen social media accounts with limitations
item.description: Meta has rolled out new features aimed at giving parents greater oversight of their children's activity on Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and Horizon. The updates include enhanced age verification us...

item.title: EACOP Pipeline Project Nears Completion, Creating Jobs and Opportunities in Tanzania
item.description: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project is now approaching its final stages, according to officials from Tanzania's Ministry of Energy. During an inspection visit to the Chongoleani area i...

item.title: Prominent Tribal Chief and Two Others Killed in Wana Market Explosion
item.description: In a tragic incident in Lower South Waziristan, at least three people were killed and four others injured when an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in Rustam Bazaar, Wana. Among the deceased...

item.title: Exxaro inaugurates new shaft at Matla Mine as Mantashe affirms coal industry’s ongoing evolution
item.description: Mineral and Petroleum Resources Minister Gwede Mantashe has reaffirmed the relevance of South Africa's coal industry amid the official opening of Exxaro Resources' New Mine 1 at Matla coal mine in Mpu...
